text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a modern software development practice. The key idea is that developers regularly merge their code changes into a shared main branch several times a day. Each time code is merged, a CI pipeline automatically starts an automated build to compile the code. Following that, a series of automated tests run to verify the new code works correctly with the existing codebase. The main purpose of this process is catching issues early, before they become major problems. By integrating frequently and running these checks, teams can quickly identify bugs, integration conflicts, or other errors, making the development process faster and more reliable.
